deduction_log.skill_name: Mount to Low Bar (Jump to LB)
deduction_log.reason: Clean mount with good body tension.
deduction_log.strength_note: Excellent initial body tension and control during the mount.

deductions.description: Slightly bent knees and relaxed toes during the circle.

deduction_log.reason: Minor form breaks in legs and feet.

deductions.description: Slightly bent knees and relaxed toes during the back hip circle.

deduction_log.reason: Consistent low cast amplitude and minor leg form breaks.

deductions.description: Cast below horizontal (approx. 170-175 degrees) before kip.
Slight pike and bent knees during the kip.

deduction_log.reason: Low cast amplitude and minor form breaks in kip.

deductions.description: Cast below horizontal (approx. 170-175 degrees) before squat on.
Heavy bar contact and slight cowboy knees during squat on.
Slight hesitation/clunky foot placement on squat on (Heavy Bar flag).

deduction_log.reason: Low cast amplitude and heavy, slightly splayed landing on squat on.
deduction_log.rule_reference: Cast amplitude, Heavy Bar flag, KTM

deductions.description: Slight pike in hips and bent knees during the jump to high bar.

deduction_log.reason: Minor form breaks in body position during bar change.

deductions.description: Slight pike in hips and bent knees during the kip.

deduction_log.reason: Minor form breaks in body position during kip.

deductions.description: Cast below horizontal (approx. 170-175 degrees) before circle.
Slightly bent knees and relaxed toes during the back hip circle.

deduction_log.reason: Consistent low cast amplitude and minor leg form breaks.

deductions.description: Cast below horizontal (approx. 170-175 degrees) before underswing.
Slightly bent knees and relaxed toes during the underswing.

deduction_log.reason: Low cast amplitude and minor leg form breaks during underswing.

deductions.description: Slight pike in hips and bent knees during the kip.

deduction_log.reason: Minor form breaks in body position during kip.

deductions.description: Cast below horizontal (approx. 170-175 degrees) before dismount.
Slight pike and cowboy knees in tuck position during flight.
Small hop on landing with chest slightly down.

deduction_log.reason: Low cast amplitude, minor form breaks in tuck, and landing deduction.

special_requirements.comment: Performed multiple kips (Low Bar Kip, Long Hang Kip).
Performed Front Hip Circle, Back Hip Circles, and Underswing.
Performed Jump to High Bar and Underswing from High Bar to Low Bar.
Performed a Tuck Flyaway dismount from the low bar. For Xcel Gold, a flight dismount from either bar is typically accepted to meet the spirit of the requirement.

artistry.notes: The routine generally flows well, but there is a lack of consistent crispness in body lines and full extension, particularly evident in the knees and toes. This impacts the overall quality of movement and body tension.

coaching_summary: This is a solid Xcel Gold routine with all special requirements met and good overall rhythm. The gymnast demonstrates a good understanding of the basic elements. However, consistent technical refinements, particularly in cast amplitude and maintaining tight body lines (knees and toes), are crucial for higher scores. The dismount is well-executed but could benefit from a cleaner landing. Focusing on these areas will significantly elevate the performance quality.
top_3_fixes: Improve Cast Amplitude: Focus on achieving a true horizontal cast (180°) on both bars. Drills should emphasize a powerful hollow-arch-hollow swing and a strong, active push away from the bar to reach the correct angle.

Maintain Leg and Foot Tension: Consistently lock knees and point toes throughout all skills and transitions. Implement 'Toe Point Monitor' and 'Knee Tension Monitor' drills, even during basic swings and casts, to build muscle memory for perfect form.

Refine Landing Control: Work on sticking landings with a stacked center of mass over the base of support. Drills should include repetition of dismounts onto firm surfaces, focusing on absorbing impact with bent knees but maintaining an upright chest and locked ankles.
celebrations: The gymnast executed a complete routine without falls or major errors.
The Mount to Low Bar was clean with excellent initial body tension.
The routine demonstrated good flow and rhythm between skills.
